items.title: ECB Made Right Choice in June Rate Hike, Lagarde Tells Les Echos
items.summary: The European Central Bank was right to raise rates when it did last month, President Christine Lagarde told Les Echos.

items.title: Unfortunately, there is a shortage of anti-ballistic capabilities – a shortage of Patriots – while Putin still intends to “vanquish” residential buildings rather than end his war. We have been discussing licenses for the production of Patriots with the U.S. Administration for a long time already. I thank all the countries helping through the PURL program, which enables us to purchase interceptors for Patriots. However, to reliably protect lives, we need our own production. European production here in Ukraine, or jointly with our partners, would also support the U.S. in operations whenever needed. We hope for a positive response from President Trump to protect lives. I also thank everyone who has expressed condolences to Ukraine today, everyone who has not remained silent and condemned Russian terror.

items.title: BlackRock’s Rick Rieder on Jobs Report, Fed Rate Cuts, Yields
items.summary: Rick Rieder, global fixed income CIO at BlackRock, says “hiring is stable, but I would say, broadly unimpressive,” as he examines the June US jobs report. He also discuses a timeframe for an interest rate hike from the Federal Reserve, the lack of forward guidance from the Fed, and where he sees yield opportunities. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Ask Gideon Rachman a question — Has the Iran war changed the world order?
items.summary: Take part in a live Ask an Expert Q&A with the FT’s chief foreign affairs commentator on Tuesday July 7 at 10.30am BST

items.title: India Plans to Expand Oil Reserves to Guard Against Price Spikes
items.summary: India plans to build larger oil inventories, expand storage capacity and deepen supply partnerships to hedge against the kind of price volatility seen during the Iran war.

items.title: It is important that the world does not remain silent about today’s utterly cynical Russian attack against our people. Thank you for your words of condolence and your willingness to help us. Defending against ballistic missiles is impossible without a sufficient number of missiles for Patriot systems. This is our greatest priority right now. We discussed which partners can help and how exactly. We will work on this. Alex noted the successes of our warriors on the front. Everyone can see how the situation is shifting in Ukraine’s favor and how effective the long-range sanctions are. This is the right kind of pressure on Russia. We are looking for formats to reinvigorate diplomacy. We exchanged views on ideas that could help and coordinated our next steps. We also discussed our bilateral defense cooperation. We are preparing to conclude important agreements in the near future. Thank you, Alex. Thank you, Finland!

items.title: Hassett Says Jobs Data Strong, Criticizes Fed's Powell
items.summary: White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett reacts to the June US employment report, saying the US jobs market is on an "upward trajectory." Speaking on "Bloomberg Open Interest," Hassett also criticizes former Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell for staying on at the central bank. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Morgan McSweeney: ‘I felt guilty about what I’d done to Keir’
items.summary: The former chief adviser to Keir Starmer forced out of his job over the Mandelson scandal talks for the first time about what went wrong

items.title: One of the sites of the Russian strike in Kyiv is an ordinary nine-story residential building. One Russian missile destroyed 64 apartments. The rubble is still being cleared at the site. All relevant agencies are working. Unfortunately, there are casualties. As of now, three people have been reported killed. My condolences to their families and loved ones. Seventeen people have been rescued. Russia has once again deliberately attacked ordinary civilians and residential buildings. In Kyiv, more than 130 such buildings have been damaged in total. It is important that our partners not simply stand by and watch as human lives are being destroyed. Pressure must be put on Russia, and it must be forced to end this aggression. It is very important to continue supporting our air defense. New contributions to the PURL program, active cooperation to build up our anti-ballistic defense – all of this is necessary to protect our people. Russia must be left with no opportunity to continue this terror. I thank all partners who stand with our people and are ready to help.

items.title: 'Most massive' Russian attack on Kyiv kills at least 20
items.summary: Kyiv's mayor declares a day of mourning after the major drone and missile attack on the Ukrainian capital.

items.title: Russia unleashes huge bombardment on Kyiv
items.summary: Attack with 74 missiles and nearly 500 drones follows Zelenskyy’s warning that Moscow was preparing ‘massive strike’

items.title: Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Iranian attack in Bahrain
items.summary: The 15-member Security Council is meeting in emergency session on Thursday morning to address the escalating tensions in the Gulf region following Iran attacks in recent days on Bahrain and Kuwait while Tehran and Washington continue to trade strikes over the Strait of Hormuz chokepoint. Stay tuned for live updates.

items.title: Jobs Report Great for Warsh and Bonds, BlackRock's Rosenberg Says
items.summary: Jeffrey Rosenberg, portfolio manager of the systematic multi-strategy fund at BlackRock, says the June jobs report will help Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh be patient on rates as well as benefit the bond market. He speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Iran Prepares to Bury Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Killed at Iran War’s Outset
items.summary: Long-delayed funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, killed during U.S.-Israeli strikes at the war’s outset, are set to begin Friday. For the regime, it is a critical moment to demonstrate that it has endured.

items.title: Bomb Explodes at Damascus Cafe, Killing at Least 6
items.summary: The blast, near the Syrian capital’s main courthouse, underscored the country’s persistent instability as its leaders try to chart a path out of years of dictatorship and war.

items.title: German prosecutors allege Ukraine ordered Nord Stream pipeline attack
items.summary: Former Ukrainian soldier accused of 'war crime' and leading a team of divers to blow up Nord Stream pipelines.

items.title: Putin Retaliates With New Strikes After Ukraine Takes the War to Russia
items.summary: Ukraine is taking the war to Russia, but so far President Vladimir V. Putin’s response has been to keep attacking, including with deadly ballistic missile and drone strikes in Kyiv on Thursday.

items.title: Are the EU and China heading for a trade war? 5 things to know
With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: Kyiv Residents Pick Up the Pieces After the Latest Russian Assault
items.summary: The latest deadly night in Ukraine, more than four years after Russia’s full-scale invasion, showed how the toll for civilians continues to mount.

items.title: Soybeans Drift as China Buying Awaited After US Tariff Statement
items.summary: Soybean futures drifted between small gains and losses as traders weighed a statement by China’s government on rolling back tariffs on US agricultural products, a move that could pave the way for purchases by the Asian country.

items.title: Russia’s Deadly Attack on Ukraine Reminds Europe of Its Own Vulnerabilities
items.summary: Governments across the continent have increased military spending, but the strikes are another warning that they need to be prepared if the conflict crosses into NATO territory.

EU and NATO Members Grapple with War in Iran

items.title: Cyberattack on Hong Kong’s Shun Hing Group affects data of 1 million people
items.summary:
A cyberattack on leading Hong Kong appliance distributor Shun Hing Group has compromised the personal information of more than 1 million people, according to the city’s privacy watchdog.
The Office of the Privacy Commissioner for Personal Data said on Thursday that it had launched an investigation into the incident after receiving a data breach report from Shun Hing Group on March 23.
